mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 320si engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW 320si (N45B20S) engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Finding a replacement engine is increasingly difficult given the limited production of 2,600 units worldwide.

The N45B20S is a hand-built homologation special engine — unique, characterful, and capable of an 8,000 rpm redline. However, the N45 family has a poor reliability reputation. Common issues include timing chain tensioner failure (often after 100,000 km), VANOS problems, oil consumption, and fuel detonation. Regular maintenance is absolutely essential.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 320si?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner failure (the defining weak point of the N45 family), oil consumption (valve stem seals and piston ring wear), VANOS system issues (idle vibration and hesitation), and fuel detonation from poor-quality fuel or oil. All are manageable with preventative maintenance and early detection.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 320si engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 320si is a future classic and one of the most affordable homologation specials money can buy. Values for good examples are rising. Against a well-maintained 320si (values £5,000–£15,000+), a £5,000–£6,500 reconditioned engine is 30–60% of the vehicle's value — often worth it for a solid example.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 320si engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code N45B20S is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 320si was produced only in 2005–2006 with 2,600 units worldwide and 500 right-hand drive examples for the UK. If your E90 is a 320si, it has the N45B20S. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain, or does it require engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner failure is repairable without replacing the engine — if caught early. A preventative chain, tensioner and guide replacement costs £800–£2,500. If the chain snaps and valves meet pistons, a full engine replacement (£4,500–£7,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure (N45 Family)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the engine on cold start, metallic clattering, engine warning light, loss of power, potential engine failure if chain skips or snaps.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles (100,000 km is a known threshold)
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£7,500 (if chain snaps and valves meet pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The N45 family is notorious for timing chain problems. The original tensioner was too short, allowing the chain to become slack and rub against the guides. By 100,000 km, the tensioner can fail completely. This is the single biggest reliability concern on the 320si. A cold-start rattle is the early warning sign — ignore it and you risk piston-valve contact and a full engine replacement. Given the rarity of the N45B20S, sourcing a replacement engine is increasingly difficult. Use only BMW-approved 5W-30 oil and reduce oil change intervals to 8,000 miles.

problems.title: Oil Consumption (N45 Family)
problems.symptoms: Low oil warning light, needing to top up oil frequently (1L per 1,000 miles or more), blue smoke from exhaust, ticking noise from engine.
problems.repairCost: £150–£300 (regular oil top-ups and monitoring) — £1,000–£3,000+ (valve stem seal or piston ring replacement)
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£7,500 (if oil starvation leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; monitor oil levels weekly

problems.recommendation: Oil consumption in the BMW N45 engine is a common issue with several potential causes, ranging from relatively minor seal failures to more significant internal wear. Worn valve stem seals, piston ring and cylinder wall wear, and crankcase ventilation system (CCV) diaphragm failure are all known causes. Check your oil level regularly — this engine is not one you can neglect. BMW strongly discourages the use of any alternative engine oil, as the consequences can be very serious.

problems.title: VANOS System Failure (N45 Family)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, hesitation on acceleration, loss of power at low RPM, reduced fuel economy, check engine light.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can affect variable valve timing

problems.recommendation: The N45B20S uses double VANOS variable valve timing. Idle vibration is a common symptom, with the most frequent cause being the VANOS system. Cleaning the VANOS unit is a common repair measure. If you experience rough idle or hesitation, have the VANOS system inspected promptly.

problems.title: Fuel Detonation & Poor Running
problems.symptoms: Engine knocking or pinging under load, loss of power, poor fuel economy, rough running.
problems.repairCost: £200–£500 (fuel system cleaning, injector service, ECU reset)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can cause engine damage if ignored

problems.recommendation: The N45 series has an obvious drawback: detonation of the fuel mixture. The problem arises from poor quality fuel or oil. BMW strongly recommends using only high-quality premium unleaded fuel and the correct grade of BMW-approved engine oil. Using the wrong oil can have very serious consequences, potentially requiring major repairs.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 320si? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with documented oil changes using BMW-approved 5W-30 oil
* No cold-start rattle — timing chain tensioner should have been replaced proactively
* No oil consumption issues — check dipstick regularly
* VANOS system functioning correctly (smooth idle, no hesitation)
* Carbon fibre valve cover intact (unique to the 320si — expensive to replace)
avoidIf: * No proof of regular oil changes — the N45B20S is unforgiving of neglect
* Cold-start rattle present — timing chain work is imminent and expensive
* Oil consumption of 1L per 1,000 miles or more — internal wear likely
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — the N45B20S can hide expensive faults
* Poor quality fuel used — detonation damage may have occurred

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW 320si is a genuine homologation special — a future classic with a hand-built engine, M Sport body kit, and motorsport DNA. It's one of the most affordable homologation specials money can buy. However, the N45B20S engine demands respect: timing chain tensioner failure, oil consumption, and VANOS issues are the key weak points. Parts availability is increasingly difficult. Buy on service history, maintain on a shortened schedule, and this is a future classic; neglect it, and you'll face expensive repairs with limited parts availability.

cta.label: → Compare BMW 320si engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 320si (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle lasting more than 1–2 seconds (timing chain tensioner warning — critical on N45 engines)
* Service History — Oil changes every 8,000 miles using BMW-approved 5W-30 oil — essential for this engine
* Oil Level — Check dipstick; excessive consumption indicates valve stem seal or piston ring wear
* VANOS Operation — Smooth idle? Any hesitation on acceleration?
* Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates cooling system issues
* Engine Running — Any knocking or pinging? Fuel detonation is a known issue
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Timing, VANOS, or fuel system codes are most common
* Carbon Fibre Valve Cover — Check for cracks or damage (unique to the 320si — expensive to replace)
